 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER

 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
71→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent?.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
71→

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 17→
 18→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 19→
 20→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 21→
 22→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 23→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 24→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 25→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 26→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 27→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 28→
 29→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 30→
 31→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 32→
 33→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 34→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 35→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 36→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 37→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 38→
 39→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 40→
 41→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 42→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 43→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 44→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 45→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 46→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 47→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 48→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 49→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 50→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 51→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 52→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 53→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 54→
 55→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 56→
 57→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 58→
 59→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 60→
 61→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 62→
 63→#### Experimental rules
 64→
 65→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 66→
 67→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 68→
 69→#### EditorConfig
 70→
 71→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 72→
 73→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 74→
 75→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 76→
 77→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 78→
 79→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 80→
 81→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 82→
 83→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 84→
 85→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 86→
 87→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 88→
 89→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 90→
 91→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 92→
 93→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
 94→
 95→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 96→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 97→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 98→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 99→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
100→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |

content.text:
I have resolved the `NullPointerException` in the `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule` that occurred when a class without a body was followed by consecutive blank lines at the end of a file.

### Root Cause Analysis
The NPE was caused by an unsafe access to `this.treeNext.elementType` in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor` function. When the whitespace node being visited is at the end of the file, `treeNext` can be `null`, leading to the exception when trying to access its `elementType`.

### Changes Made
- **`NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule.kt`**: Added safe calls (`?.`) to `treeParent` and `treeNext` in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor` function. This ensures that if the node is at the end of the file (and thus has no next sibling), the check safely evaluates to `false` instead of throwing an NPE.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Added an entry under the `Fixed` section for the unreleased version to document this fix.
- **`NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleReproductionTest.kt`**: Created a new test file with a reproduction case that specifically targets the scenario described in the issue, ensuring it now correctly reports a lint violation and formats the code without crashing.

### Verification Results
- The reproduction test case passed successfully.
- All existing tests in the `ktlint-ruleset-standard` module passed, ensuring no regressions were introduced.
- The fix correctly handles the reported case and prevents the `Internal Error` (NPE) in Ktlint.

14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId

 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent?.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 17→
 18→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 19→
 20→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 21→
 22→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 23→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 24→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 25→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 26→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 27→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 28→
 29→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 30→
 31→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 32→
 33→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 34→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 35→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 36→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 37→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 38→
 39→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 40→
 41→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 42→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 43→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 44→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 45→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 46→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 47→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 48→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 49→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 50→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 51→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 52→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 53→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 54→
 55→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 56→
 57→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 58→
 59→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 60→
 61→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 62→
 63→#### Experimental rules
 64→
 65→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 66→
 67→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 68→
 69→#### EditorConfig
 70→
 71→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 72→
 73→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 74→
 75→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 76→
 77→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 78→
 79→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 80→
 81→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 82→
 83→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 84→
 85→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 86→
 87→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 88→
 89→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 90→
 91→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 92→
 93→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
 94→
 95→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 96→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 97→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 98→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 99→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
100→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
